input.prompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

input.initialPrompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check dotnet availability and binlog file

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build binary log (binlog) file to diagnose build failures. Let me first examine the binlog file and use appropriate tools to extract information from it.

Binary log files (.binlog) are MSBuild structured log files. I can use the `dotnet build` tool or the MSBuild Structured Log Viewer CLI to analyze them. Let me first check what tools are available and then try to extract error information from the binlog.

Let me try a few approaches:
1. Use `dotnet msbuild` to replay the binlog
2. Use the MSBuild structured log viewer CLI tool


3. Try to extract text from the binary file

The most common approach is to use `dotnet build -flp:logfile=output.log` to replay, or use the `MSBuild.StructuredLogger` tool. Let me start by checking if dotnet is available and then try to replay or analyze the binlog.

arguments.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; ls -la build.binlog", "description": "Check dotnet availability and binlog file", "initial_wait": 10}

toolArgs.description: Check dotnet availability and binlog file

arguments.command: dotnet tool install --global MSBuild.StructuredLogger.Tool 2>/dev/null || true; export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools"; dotnet-structuredlogviewer 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool install --global dotnet-msbuildlog 2>/dev/null || true

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet tool install --global MSBuild.StructuredLogger.Tool 2>/dev/null || true; export PATH=\"$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools\"; dotnet-structuredlogviewer 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool install --global dotnet-msbuildlog 2>/dev/null || true", "description": "Install structured log viewer tool", "initial_wait": 30}

toolArgs.command: dotnet tool install --global MSBuild.StructuredLogger.Tool 2>/dev/null || true; export PATH="$PATH:$HOME/.dotnet/tools"; dotnet-structuredlogviewer 2>/dev/null || dotnet tool install --global dotnet-msbuildlog 2>/dev/null || true

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Try replaying the binlog to extract errors and warnings
dotnet build build.binlog 2>&1 | head -200
arguments.description: Replay binlog to see build output

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Replay binlog to see build output

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Check if Api project also failed or was skipped due to dependency on Services
dotnet build build.binlog 2>&1 | grep -i -E "(error|warning|skipped|not build)" | grep -i api; echo "---"; dotnet build build.binlog 2>&1 | grep -i "build succeeded\|build failed\|did not build"
arguments.description: Check if Api project was affected

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check if Api project was affected
